project.name: almaapitk
project.description: Python toolkit for the Ex Libris Alma ILS (Integrated Library System) REST API: an HTTP client (AlmaAPIClient) plus domain classes (Acquisitions, Admin, Analytics, BibliographicRecords, Configuration, Electronic, ResourceSharing, Users), response wrapping, an error hierarchy, a shared paginator, and API-key-redacting logging. Surrounded by a chunk-driven agentic implementation pipeline, a layered test suite, and design/operational docs.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: The sole supported public API surface for the almaapitk package (contract v0.2.0); resolves __version__ from installed distribution metadata and lazily imports all public symbols (client, response, error hierarchy, domain classes, utilities) from almaapitk._internal on first access to avoid circular imports.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PEP 562 module-level __getattr__ for lazy symbol loading; reads version via importlib.metadata to stay in sync with pyproject.toml.

nodes.name: __getattr__
nodes.summary: Module-level lazy-import handler that resolves a requested public symbol via the _lazy_imports map, importing its module on first access and caching the result; raises AttributeError for unknown names.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel/package init for the optional workflow smoke-test harness (installed via almaapitk[smoke]); re-exports the harness public surface — build_smoke_client, RecordingTransport/RecordedCall, the read-only guard, smoke-input loader, flaky-tolerance helpers, and the @workflow marker.

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: Assembles an AlmaAPIClient wired for a workflow smoke: installs a RecordingTransport in dry-run (no network) and a read-only guard last (so dry-run writes are still violations), returning the (client, transport) pair.

nodes.name: build_smoke_client
nodes.summary: Builds an AlmaAPIClient for the given environment, installing a RecordingTransport on the session in dry-run mode and the read-only guard when readonly is set; returns (client, transport) where transport is None outside dry-run.

nodes.name: flaky.py
nodes.summary: Tolerates transient Alma API hiccups (5xx/429, e.g. analytics 500 storms) during live smokes by retrying and, if still failing, raising TransientAPIError so callers can treat the check as skipped rather than failed.

nodes.name: TransientAPIError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subclass signalling that a live check could not complete due to a transient API failure; callers translate it into a skipped (not failed) result.

nodes.name: run_with_flaky_tolerance
nodes.summary: Calls a zero-arg callable and retries it on transient API errors (AlmaServerError/AlmaRateLimitError) with a delay; returns the result on success, re-raises non-transient errors unchanged, and raises TransientAPIError if every attempt failed transiently.

nodes.name: guards.py
nodes.summary: Read-only rail for smoke clients: wraps a requests.Session so any non-GET verb raises ReadOnlyViolation before any I/O, making the 'PRODUCTION is read-only, always' invariant enforceable rather than aspirational.

nodes.name: ReadOnlyViolation
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subclass raised when a read-only (e.g. PRODUCTION-targeted) smoke attempts a write request.

nodes.name: install_readonly_guard
nodes.summary: Monkeypatches session.request with a guard that raises ReadOnlyViolation on any non-GET verb and otherwise delegates to the original request method, enforcing read-only access on the session.

nodes.name: inputs.py
nodes.summary: Loads synthetic smoke inputs (test user id, analytics report path, etc.) from a gitignored smoke-data.json (path overridable via ALMA_SMOKE_DATA) so real identifiers never reach the repo (R9), raising MissingTestInput with actionable guidance when the file or key is absent.

nodes.name: MissingTestInput
nodes.summary: KeyError subclass raised when a requested smoke input value, or the smoke-data file itself, is missing.

nodes.name: smoke_input
nodes.summary: Returns the synthetic value for a key from the gitignored smoke-data JSON file, raising MissingTestInput with copy-the-example guidance when the file is absent or the key is not present.

nodes.name: pytest_plugin.py
nodes.summary: pytest plugin (registered as a pytest11 entry point so it auto-loads with almaapitk[smoke]) exposing the `alma` fixture; selects live vs dry-run via ALMA_SMOKE_LIVE, skips live checks lacking credentials, and builds the smoke client from the test's @workflow marker.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposed via setuptools pytest11 entry point; the `alma` pytest fixture yields the client and closes it in a finally block.

nodes.name: alma
nodes.summary: pytest fixture that reads the test's @workflow metadata, fails if undecorated, skips live runs lacking the environment's API key, builds a dry-run or live smoke client (placeholder key in dry-run), stashes transport/live context on the node, and yields the client (closed on teardown).

nodes.name: transport.py
nodes.summary: Dry-run transport for the smoke harness: replaces Session.request with a recorder that performs no I/O, appends each attempt to a RecordedCall list, and returns a canned requests.Response (default empty HTTP 200 JSON, overridable per workflow) so the workflow code keeps running.

nodes.name: RecordedCall
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing one request a workflow attempted under dry-run: method, url, and optional params, headers, and body.

nodes.name: RecordingTransport
nodes.summary: Installs a no-I/O recorder over a requests.Session that logs every request as a RecordedCall and returns a canned response from a configurable factory (default empty 200 JSON), enabling credential-free, network-free validation of workflow wiring.

nodes.name: workflow.py
nodes.summary: Defines the @workflow decorator marking a pytest test as a workflow smoke; it records name/environment/readonly metadata on the function (read later by the `alma` fixture) and enforces that PRODUCTION workflows must be readonly.

nodes.name: workflow
nodes.summary: Decorator factory that validates PRODUCTION workflows are readonly (else ValueError) and attaches a __alma_workflow__ dict (name, environment, readonly) to the decorated test function for the `alma` fixture to consume.

nodes.name: domains.py
nodes.summary: Internal re-export hub that aggregates all eight Alma domain classes (Admin, Users, BibliographicRecords, Acquisitions, ResourceSharing, Analytics, Configuration, Electronic) for the package's public namespace; not meant for direct import.
nodes.languageNotes: Python internal namespace module: pure import-and-__all__ aggregation, no logic.

nodes.name: AlmaValidationError
nodes.summary: Exception subclass raised for client-side validation failures (bad parameters, missing required fields) before a request is sent.

nodes.name: AlmaAuthenticationError
nodes.summary: Typed exception raised on HTTP 401 authentication failures (issue #9 error taxonomy).

nodes.name: AlmaRateLimitError
nodes.summary: Typed exception raised on HTTP 429 rate-limit responses (issue #9 error taxonomy).

nodes.name: AlmaServerError
nodes.summary: Typed exception raised on 5xx server-side failures from the Alma API (issue #9 error taxonomy).

nodes.name: AlmaResourceNotFoundError
nodes.summary: Typed exception raised on HTTP 404 when a requested Alma resource does not exist (issue #9 error taxonomy).

nodes.name: AlmaInvalidPolModeError

nodes.name: CredentialError

nodes.name: AlmaAPIClient
nodes.summary: Environment-aware HTTP client for the Alma REST API: sets up a persistent requests.Session with retry/backoff, loads region host and API key from env, builds and dispatches requests through a single _request chokepoint with logging and error classification, exposes get/post/put/delete and an iter_paged generator, and supports context-manager cleanup.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralises all HTTP verbs through _request; mounts an HTTPAdapter with urllib3 Retry; iter_paged is a generator that fetches Alma pages on demand honouring limit/offset/total_record_count and an optional max_records cap.

nodes.summary: Domain class for Alma Sets administration: validates set types, reads and paginates set members (USER/BIB_MMS), extracts member IDs from response links, fetches set metadata with processing estimates and warnings, lists sets with optional member counts, and performs create/update/delete plus add/remove-member operations through the AlmaAPIClient.

nodes.name: handlers.py

nodes.name: logger.py

nodes.name: TextFormatter

nodes.name: _partial_redact_id
nodes.summary: Partially masks an identifier by keeping only a few leading/trailing characters, so logs remain debuggable without exposing full IDs.

nodes.name: redact_url_ids

nodes.name: redact_sensitive_data
nodes.summary: Recursively walks dict/list/string structures redacting secret-keyed values (api keys, tokens, passwords) and partially masking identifier fields; the central scrubbing routine used by both formatters.

nodes.name: AlmaRotatingFileHandler
nodes.summary: RotatingFileHandler subclass that ensures the target log directory exists before opening the file and applies size-based rotation defaults.

nodes.name: DateOrganizedFileHandler

nodes.name: create_log_directory_structure

nodes.name: AlmaLogger

layers.name: Core Library
layers.description: The importable almaapitk package: AlmaAPIClient HTTP transport, Alma domain classes (Acquisitions, Users, Bibs, Admin, Analytics, Configuration, Electronic, ResourceSharing), the alma_logging redaction framework, TSV/citation utils, and the consumer testing harness.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest coverage for the library: unit tests for domains/utils, regression tests, client integration tests, logging tests, agentic-pipeline tests, smoke/contract tests, and meta dependency checks.

layers.name: Agentic Pipeline & Tooling
layers.description: Operational scripts: the chunk-driven implementation CLI (scripts/agentic), Alma error-code tooling, post-publish/CI helpers, and investigation/smoke-import utilities.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Design specs, operator playbooks, domain/API guides, release and manual-QA docs, slash-command definitions, plus root CHANGELOG/README/CLAUDE guidance.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Config
layers.description: Build, CI/CD, and project configuration: GitHub Actions CI/release pipelines, the Makefile smoke targets, pyproject.toml packaging, guardrails.json policy, and logging/smoke config templates.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what almaapitk is: a Python toolkit that wraps the Ex Libris Alma ILS REST API behind a clean, importable library. It lays out the feature set, installation, a quick-start example, and an API reference for the core client and the domain classes you'll meet throughout this tour. Read this first to get the project's purpose and vocabulary before opening any code.

tour.title: The Public API Barrel
tour.description: The package's single supported entry point is src/almaapitk/__init__.py — the contract that consumers import from. It resolves the package __version__ from installed distribution metadata and lazily imports every public symbol (the client, response wrapper, error hierarchy, domain classes, and utilities) on first access. Understanding this barrel tells you exactly what surface the library promises and how it stays stable.
tour.languageLesson: Python modules can define __getattr__ at module level (PEP 562) to lazily resolve attributes on first access. almaapitk uses this to expose a flat public API while deferring heavy imports from an internal package, avoiding circular-import problems.

tour.title: The HTTP Client Core
tour.description: AlmaAPIClient is the foundation every domain builds on, and the most depended-upon file in the codebase. It carries a persistent requests.Session with urllib3 retry/backoff, environment-aware credential loading (SANDBOX vs PRODUCTION), the AlmaResponse wrapper, the full typed exception hierarchy, error classification, a shared paginator (iter_paged), and the get/post/put/delete verb helpers. Master this and the Client-Domain pattern that follows becomes obvious.
tour.languageLesson: iter_paged is a Python generator: it yields one record at a time and only fetches the next page when the consumer asks for it. Callers that break out early (e.g. find-first) skip the remaining network calls — lazy evaluation as a performance feature.

tour.title: Core Domains: Users & Bibs
tour.description: Domain classes are thin wrappers that take an AlmaAPIClient and expose Alma operations for one area. Users covers broad Users-API coverage — user CRUD, fees, loans, requests, attachments, and the expired-email bulk workflow — while BibliographicRecords handles bib/holdings/items CRUD, MARC field editing, scan-in, and digital representations. Together they show how a domain delegates all transport to the client from Step 3 and focuses purely on endpoint shape and validation.

tour.title: Acquisitions & Resource Sharing
tour.description: Building on the same Client-Domain pattern, Acquisitions implements the invoice create/approve/pay workflows plus POL search and item receiving, and is a primary user of the client's iter_paged paginator. ResourceSharing wraps the Partners API to create and read lending requests, including a citation-driven request builder that foreshadows the citation utility in Step 8. These two are among the most heavily depended-upon domains in production consumer repos.

tour.title: Admin & Analytics
tour.description: Admin provides full Sets management over the Conf API — reading USER and BIB_MMS members, paginating member IDs, and full CRUD with add/remove. Analytics fetches report headers and rows, parsing Alma's XML rowset responses and following ResumptionToken pagination (note: Analytics requires PRODUCTION credentials, since Alma exposes a single shared analytics DB). These round out the established domains before we reach the newer foundation skeletons.

tour.title: Foundation Domains: Configuration & Electronic
tour.description: These two show the toolkit's expansion frontier. Configuration is a broad wrapper over the Conf API — org structure, locations CRUD, code/mapping tables, profiles, letters/printers — landed via the coverage backlog (issue #22). Electronic is still a foundation skeleton (issue #66) offering only environment introspection and a smoke connection check, with e-collection/e-service/portfolio methods to come. Contrasting a mature domain with a bootstrap one reveals how new API coverage is grown incrementally.

tour.title: Logging & Redaction Framework
tour.description: alma_logging is a cross-cutting subsystem every domain and the client pull in. The package barrel re-exports get_logger, AlmaLogger, formatters, handlers, and config so consumers import everything from one place; the handlers module supplies size-based rotating and date-organized file handlers that write into per-day directories. Its defining feature is automatic API-key/token redaction, so structured logs are safe to keep — the project mandates this logger over print() everywhere.

tour.title: Utilities: TSV & Citation Metadata
tour.description: The utils subpackage holds standalone helpers that don't need an Alma client. TSVGenerator produces TSV files for data export, while citation_metadata fetches and normalizes article metadata from free public sources (PubMed E-utilities by PMID, Crossref by DOI) with its own error hierarchy — this is what powers ResourceSharing's citation-driven request builder from Step 5. These show how the library composes external-data enrichment alongside the core Alma surface.

tour.title: Optional Smoke-Test Harness
tour.description: Installed via the almaapitk[smoke] extra, the testing subpackage is a harness for verifying real workflows safely. Its barrel re-exports build_smoke_client, a RecordingTransport/RecordedCall pair, a read-only guard, a smoke-input loader, flaky-tolerance helpers, and a @workflow marker. It lets consumer repos run read-only live checks (or fully mocked dry-runs) against Alma without risking writes — the safety layer that lets the team bump production consumers with confidence.

tour.title: The Public-API Contract Test
tour.description: test_public_api_contract.py is the guardrail behind the barrel from Step 2: it asserts that almaapitk keeps exposing its documented client, response, error, and domain symbols with stable import surfaces. It's one of the most-referenced files in the test layer because it codifies the package's promise to downstream consumers. This is the layered test suite's contract tier — unit tests for domains/utils, regression tests, and integration tests sit beneath it.
tour.languageLesson: Contract tests assert on the import surface itself (what symbols exist and where), not behavior. They catch accidental API breakage — a renamed or moved public symbol — before it reaches PyPI consumers, complementing the behavioral unit and integration tests.

tour.title: Tooling, CI & Build
tour.description: Surrounding the library is its operational machinery. scripts/agentic/chunks is the bash operator CLI that drives the chunk-based implementation pipeline (and enforces R8 by refusing to run when a prod key is set). pyproject.toml packages the distribution and declares the requests dependency plus the [smoke] extra; the Makefile wraps dry-run and read-only live smoke targets; and the GitHub Actions ci.yml gates every push with a wheel/sdist contents check and a bandit security scan. This is how the code from Steps 2-11 gets validated, packaged, and shipped.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use 'on' triggers to fire jobs on push/PR; each job runs sequential steps. Here CI acts as a release gate — packaging and security checks must pass before merge, complementing the tag-triggered release pipeline.
